maxmemory_policy.desc: Sets the desired key eviction behavior when a Redis instance reaches max memory.

values.noeviction: return errors when memory limit is reached
values.allkeys-lru: evict keys trying to remove the less recently used keys first
values.volatile-lru: evict keys trying to remove the less recently used keys first, but only those that have an expiry set
values.volatile-random: evict random keys in order to make space for the new data added, but only evict keys with an expire set
values.volatile-ttl: evict only keys with an expire set, and try to evict keys with a shorter time to live (TTL) first
values.allkeys-lfu: evict any key using approximated LFU
values.volatile-lfu: evict using approximated LFU among the keys with an expire set

timeout.desc: Represent how long a client can idle before being disconnected.

standby_segv_workaround.desc: There's a known segv bug with redis: https://trello.com/c/zW04yt19/551-known-redis-segfaults. We can improve the ability of standbys to handle that by setting particular redis settings.
